title: Past Events I
scenario: talking about what you did yesterday

title: Experience
scenario: talking about things you have done before

title: Ability and Learning
scenario: talking about what you can do and are learning

title: Invitations and Rescheduling
scenario: making, accepting, declining, and changing plans

title: Housing II
scenario: talking to landlord or repair person

title: Problem Explanation II
scenario: asking for help after a problem

title: Obligation and Advice
scenario: what should or must be done

title: Conditional Plans
scenario: if this happens, then do that

title: Contrast and Concession
scenario: although something is true, another thing is also true

title: Direction Complements I
scenario: movement in and out, up and down

title: Direction Complements II
scenario: bringing, taking, coming back, going back

title: Storytelling II
scenario: telling stories with interruption and questions

title: Phone Calls
scenario: calling a shop, clinic, or friend

title: Messaging
scenario: short chat messages in Traditional Chinese

title: Explaining Background
scenario: where you are from, why you are learning, what you do

title: Extended Social Conversation
scenario: meeting a local and chatting for several minutes

title: Integrated Scenario I
scenario: arrive at a new place, ask directions, buy something, make small talk

title: Integrated Scenario II
scenario: health or housing problem from start to resolution

title: Integrated Scenario III
scenario: social plan with conflict and rescheduling

title: Final Communication Assessment
scenario: 90-day final speaking, listening, reading, and writing checkpoint
